title: Upholstery stain paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate-of-soda and castile-soap paste for spot-cleaning sofas, chairs, and car seats
excerpt: Apply, leave to lift the stain, then scrub and blot off. Bicarbonate of soda deodourises and absorbs; castile soap breaks down grease and water-soluble marks. Suitable for most woven upholstery fabrics.
sourceNotes: Surfactant action on fabric stains: standard textile chemistry. Bicarbonate of soda as an absorbent and deodouriser: household chemistry reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Make a small batch and top up as needed; the paste thickens over time as the water in the soap evaporates.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps two months in a sealed jar. Stir before use if the paste has stiffened.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within two months. Discard if the paste develops a rancid or sour smell, or if it separates and will not re-combine on stirring.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium bicarbonate -- a mild alkali that neutralises acidic odour compounds and absorbs liquid from upholstery fibres while the paste sits on the stain.

content.text: This paste lifts soft-furnishing stains in two stages. The castile soap acts as a surfactant, breaking the stain's bond with the fabric fibres, while
absorbs the loosened material and deodourises the fabric at the same time. Works well on food and drink marks, pet accidents (after blotting up the worst), and general sofa grime.

items.prepNote: about 30 ml; adjust to reach a thick paste

content.text: Place the bicarbonate of soda in a small jar. Add the castile soap a little at a time, stirring after each addition. The mixture will fizz briefly as the soap meets the bicarb; keep stirring until it settles into a smooth paste. Adjust to a thick, spreadable consistency -- firm enough to cling to a vertical surface without running.
Stir in 10 drops of lavender if using. This adds a mild deodourising scent to the finished fabric.

First blot up any excess liquid with a clean cloth -- do not rub. Spread a generous layer of paste over the stained area and work it gently into the fibres with a soft brush or the back of a spoon. Leave the paste for 10 to 30 minutes so the bicarbonate can absorb the stain. Then scrub gently with a damp cloth in a circular motion and blot dry. Repeat once if the stain remains. Allow the area to dry fully before sitting on it.

Test on a hidden seam or underside before treating visible areas. Safe on most woven cotton and synthetic upholstery fabrics. Not for velvet, suede, leather, or silk -- these require specialist cleaning. Check the sofa label: W or WS codes indicate water-safe cleaning; S-only codes mean solvent only, and water-based products will mark them.
Keep in a sealed jar at room temperature. Use within two months. Stir before use. Discard if the paste separates completely or develops a sour smell.
